A New Standard for California Cities
The state says approve it.
Now cities can prove why they can't.
How one number changes the housing mandate calculus — permanently.
Methodology developed with Worcester Polytechnic Institute · Fire Safety Engineering · Beta
Why This Approach Start with First Principles
California Stewardship Alliance · JOSH 2 / 10
Design Goals Start with First Principles
01
Survive Appellate Review
Written in advance, uniformly applied, traceable to authoritative public sources. Designed to win in court — not just in council chambers.
02
Physical Capacity
Grounded in the physical reality of how many vehicles a road can move before fire overtakes it. Roads have carrying capacity. That capacity is measurable.
03
An Objective Standard That Can't Be Argued
Simple arithmetic. Every input is public. Every step traceable by hand. No discretion. No black box. No room for argument.
The Principle You already know this rule
California Stewardship Alliance · JOSH 3 / 10
🚪
The Building
A fire marshal sets maximum occupancy based on exit capacity.
That number is not a preference. It is not discretionary. It is the math of how many people can get through the door before they die.

Nobody argues with it. It is simply law.
🏔️
The Evacuation Corridor
A wildfire evacuation road is a door. It has a carrying capacity.
When fire comes, every resident behind that road needs to clear it within a defined time window.

Add more people than the road can carry — and the math stops working. People don't get out.
The same principle. The same math. A standard cities have never had for roads.
The Standard Delta-T · ΔT
California Stewardship Alliance · JOSH 4 / 10
The Metric
ΔT
Delta-T · minutes
Any project adding more than
5% to corridor load
is flagged as discretionary.
The marginal increase in evacuation time caused by adding one project's residents to a corridor already under stress.
A fire marshal doesn't ask who is already inside before deciding if fifty more people can safely enter. The question is whether those fifty can get through the door fast enough.

ΔT asks the same question — at the scale of a wildfire evacuation road. In minutes. Derived from NFPA 101. Auditable by any judge.
The Legal Weight
ΔT is not an opinion. It is not a city's judgment. It is a number — grounded in federal life-safety standards — that no mandate can make mathematically false.
Real Project · Encinitas · Sage Canyon Apartments 120 Units · Zone 3 Very High FHSZ
California Stewardship Alliance · JOSH 5 / 10
ΔT · Marginal Evacuation Time
48.6
minutes
Limit: 2.25 min  ·  21.6× over threshold
The Controlling Finding
This project adds 48.6 minutes to a corridor with a 2.25-minute safety limit.
Under current law — without ΔT as an adopted standard — this project may be entitled to ministerial approval. The developer's argument writes itself: the road was already failing before we submitted. You cannot hold us responsible.

ΔT makes that argument mathematically irrelevant. The marginal question is all that matters.
JOSH Verdict
Discretionary. Planning Commission review required. The math is not disputable — and neither is the standard that produced it.
Before Your City Attorney Asks Why ΔT Survives Appellate Review
California Stewardship Alliance · JOSH 6 / 10
First Principles Designed from the ground up to survive the hardest legal challenge Sacramento can mount.
1
Written & Pre-Existing
The standard exists before any project is submitted — published, versioned, uniformly available. No post-hoc judgment. The HAA's own language requires exactly this structure.
2
Expert-Verified Public Data
Every input drawn from authoritative public sources: NFPA 101, NIST Camp Fire analysis, Cal Fire zone data, federal HCM traffic methodology. Not city-invented numbers — sources no attorney can credibly attack.
3
Simple Arithmetic
Every ΔT calculation is deterministic — no AI, no black box, no proprietary model. A city engineer, an opposing attorney, or a judge can trace every step by hand. Full audit trail with every output.
4
Built for Appellate Review
Project-specific nexus. Uniform application. Objective threshold. The exact properties courts have upheld under the HAA health and safety carve-out. This structure was designed to win.
JOSH · Jurisdictional Objective Standards for Housing Running on a real project · Encinitas
This is the tool. Open source. Auditable. Ready. 7 / 10
The Output Discretionary · Planning Commission review required
Every step auditable. Every number sourced. Every finding defensible in court. 8 / 10
The Coalition One city is a target. Twenty cities is a standard.
California Stewardship Alliance · JOSH 9 / 10
Why This Scales
Sacramento can challenge one city.
It cannot challenge a standard.
When twenty cities adopt the same ΔT methodology, the same ordinance language, and the same open-source tool — it is no longer one city's invention. It is a coalition's standard, validated by independent researchers, grounded in federal law.

Every city that joins makes it harder to attack. Every precedent shared makes the next city safer.
Research Validation
Methodology developed in collaboration with Worcester Polytechnic Institute — world leader in fire safety engineering. Independent validation in progress.

Objective standards. Defensible methodology. Cities as stewards. 10 / 10
The Invitation
What is your city's number?
No commitment. No budget. One conversation — and you'll know exactly where your evacuation corridors stand, what projects are in your pipeline, and what your legal options are.

We'll run the analysis. You decide what to do with it.
1
Request a Briefing
One meeting. We run your city's JOSH analysis and walk through the results with you and your staff.
2
Review with Your Attorney
Share the analysis and legal memo with your city attorney. Everything is open source and fully auditable.
3
Join the Coalition
Adopt the model ordinance. Your city's weight makes the standard harder to attack for every city that follows.
